info.title: trunfold
info.description:
Copyright (c) 2023 Ken Domino
# trunfold
## Summary
Perform an unfold transform on a grammar
## Description
The unfold command applies the unfold transform to a collection of terminal
nodes
in the parse tree, which is identified with the supplied xpath expression. Prior
to using this command, you must have the file parsed. An unfold operation
substitutes
the right-hand side of a parser or lexer rule into a reference of the rule name
that
occurs at the specified node.
## Usage
trunfold <string>
## Examples
Before:
grammar Expresion;
s : e ;
e : e '*' e       # Mult
| INT           # primary
;
INT : [0-9]+ ;
WS : [ \t\n]+ -> skip ;
